quick_skim.title: A Single-Cell Atlas Reveals Cancer Cell Plasticity and Stromal-Immune Remodeling in Uterine Carcinosarcoma Across Diverse Ancestries
quick_skim.abstract: Uterine carcinosarcoma (UCS) is an aggressive endometrial cancer defined by coexisting malignant epithelial and mesenchymal components, rapid metastatic dissemination, and poor therapeutic response. However, its cellular ecosystem remains poorly resolved, particularly in patients of African ancestry who are underrepresented in genomic datasets despite a disproportionate disease burden. Here, we generated a single-cell atlas of 15 primary and metastatic UCS specimens from a diverse cohort of 13 patients enriched for African ancestry, integrated with whole-genome sequencing. Malignant cells exhibited epithelial-like, mesenchymal-like, transitional, and stem/progenitor-like states within individual tumors that mapped to patient-specific copy number-defined subclones and RNA-velocity trajectories, supporting metaplastic state transitions. Compared to normal endometrium, primary tumors were enriched for epithelial-mesenchymal-transition (EMT), mTORC1, and glycolytic programs, whereas matched metastases show enhanced TNF-NF{kappa}B-associated invasive programs. The tumor microenvironment contained immunosuppressive myeloid states and diverse cancer-associated fibroblast (CAF) subsets, including pericyte-like and matrix-remodeling subsets that act as predicted communication hubs through chemokine and immune-checkpoint circuits. We found a CAF-centered CCL2-CXCL1/2-IL10 module linked to CD8 T-cell dysfunction and a TIGIT-CD96-PVR checkpoint module. These data define the UCS cellular ecosystem in which malignant plasticity is coupled to stromal-immune cell remodeling in a patient cohort of enriched ancestries and nominate stromal-immune axes for further therapeutic investigation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Single-cell atlas reveals cancer cell plasticity and stromal-immune remodeling in a therapy-resistant tumor
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 单细胞图谱揭示治疗耐药肿瘤中癌细胞可塑性和基质-免疫重塑
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 单细胞图谱揭示治疗耐药肿瘤中癌细胞可塑性和基质-免疫重塑
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 单细胞图谱揭示治疗耐药肿瘤中癌细胞可塑性和基质-免疫重塑
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study generates a single-cell atlas of uterine carcinosarcoma, uncovering cancer cell plasticity and stromal-immune remodeling linked to poor therapeutic response.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 子宫癌肉瘤侵袭性强且治疗反应差，但其细胞生态系统尚不明确。本文对15个原发和转移样本进行单细胞测序，整合全基因组测序，构建了包含多种癌细胞状态（上皮样、间质样、过渡态和干/祖细胞样）的图谱。揭示了癌细胞可塑性和基质-免疫重塑在治疗抵抗中的作用。该资源为理解肿瘤异质性和耐药机制提供了单细胞维度见解。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 子宫癌肉瘤侵袭性强且治疗反应差，但其细胞生态系统尚不明确。本文对15个原发和转移样本进行单细胞测序，整合全基因组测序，构建了包含多种癌细胞状态（上皮样、间质样、过渡态和干/祖细胞样）的图谱。揭示了癌细胞可塑性和基质-免疫重塑在治疗抵抗中的作用。该资源为理解肿瘤异质性和耐药机制提供了单细胞维度见解。

quick_skim.title: TOPAS: phosphoproteome data analysis and decision support platform for molecular tumor boards
quick_skim.abstract: The molecular tumor board (MTB) is central to precision oncology, providing personalized treatment recommendations based on molecular profiles of patient tumors. Genomics is instrumental for MTBs but often fails to identify clinically actionable targets, a gap that phosphoproteomics can fill. We present the tumor proteome activity status (TOPAS) platform, an end to end analysis pipeline that converts terabytes of phosphoproteomic data into patient-specific reports for MTB discussions, focusing on clinically relevant signaling linked to oncogenic mechanisms and therapeutic targets. Designed to scale with growing cohorts, the platform integrates data from 1,998 tumor samples to support patient- and cohort-level hypothesis generation. A web portal handles quality control, calculates TOPAS scores, identifies tumor antigens and immune checkpoints, and offers interactive analyses of differential protein abundance and outlier detection. The TOPAS platform is open source, addresses a critical unmet need and facilitates broader adoption of phosphoproteomics in precision oncology in the future.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: bioinformatics platform for drug resistance analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生物信息学平台可用于耐药分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生物信息学平台可用于耐药分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生物信息学平台可用于耐药分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TOPAS platform converts phosphoproteomic data into patient-specific reports for molecular tumor boards, integrating 1,998 tumor samples.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 分子肿瘤委员会依赖基因组学，但常缺乏可靶向改变。本文介绍TOPAS平台，一个端到端分析流程，可将海量磷酸化蛋白质组数据转化为患者特异性报告，聚焦临床相关信号。平台整合了1998个肿瘤样本，支持患者和队列级别的假设生成，为将磷酸化蛋白质组学纳入精准肿瘤学实践提供了可扩展的工具。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: bioinformatics analysis of drug resistance in neoadjuvant therapy

quick_skim.title: A Bayesian Network-Based Framework for Causal Cancer Drug Target Discovery Integrating Patient and Cell Line Data
quick_skim.abstract: Current approaches to cancer drug target discovery face two key limitations: poor translation of cell line-derived targets to patient tumors, and the lack of causal explanation of the regulatory mechanisms underlying target prioritization. Here we present BayesTx (Bayesian Therapeutics target discovery), a Bayesian network framework that integrates patient transcriptomics data with cell line data to identify causal therapeutic targets in cancer. BayesTx projects both data domains into a shared biological space of pathway and transcription factor activities, learns domain-specific causal graphs, and merges them through weighted edge aggregation with bootstrap consensus filtering. Do-simulation on the consensus network quantifies the causal effect of each transcription factor on cancer cell viability. Applied to breast cancer using TCGA-BRCA (The Cancer Genome Atlas breast cancer cohort) and DepMap (Cancer Dependency Map) datasets, the framework ranked 47 transcription factors by predicted causal impact, with gene-level targets further derived through regulon-based propagation. Top-ranked transcription factor (TF) targets were independently supported by survival analysis in external cohort data and pharmacogenomic drug response associations. Overall, BayesTx demonstrates that cross-domain Bayesian network modeling can bridge patient and cell line data to systematically identify causal therapeutic targets in cancer.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Bayesian network for causal drug target discovery integrating patient data
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 整合患者数据的贝叶斯网络因果药物靶点发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 整合患者数据的贝叶斯网络因果药物靶点发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 整合患者数据的贝叶斯网络因果药物靶点发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BayesTx integrates patient transcriptomics with cell line data via Bayesian networks to identify causal cancer drug targets, applicable to resistance target discovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究提出BayesTx框架，利用贝叶斯网络整合患者转录组数据和细胞系数据，学习因果图以鉴定癌症药物靶点。该方法可因果推断耐药相关靶点，直接应用于生物信息学分析新辅助治疗耐药机制，为耐药靶点发现提供因果推理工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究提出BayesTx框架，利用贝叶斯网络整合患者转录组数据和细胞系数据，学习因果图以鉴定癌症药物靶点。该方法可因果推断耐药相关靶点，直接应用于生物信息学分析新辅助治疗耐药机制，为耐药靶点发现提供因果推理工具。

quick_skim.title: Genetically distinct microenvironment determines cancer survival and response to therapy in mice
quick_skim.abstract: Genetic uniqueness of the tumor microenvironment significantly influences cancer growth, survival, and response to therapy, independent of the cancer cell's intrinsic properties or the adaptive immune system. Using genetically distinct Rag1-/- mouse models, this study shows that different strains exhibit varied tumor growth kinetics and survival outcomes when xenografted with identical leukemic and solid tumor cell lines. This study further highlights the critical role of the myeloid immune compartment and shows that disrupting both lymphoid and myeloid systems alters cancer progression. These results also reveal that the tumor microenvironment can permanently alter cancer cell phenotypes and significantly affect chemotherapy efficacy, as seen with Cisplatin's varying effects across strains. These findings underscore the importance of considering genetic background in preclinical cancer models, suggesting that reliance upon a single mouse strain may lead to incomplete conclusions about cancer biology and treatment efficacy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: genetic uniqueness of tumor microenvironment influences chemotherapy response
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 肿瘤微环境遗传独特性影响化疗反应
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 肿瘤微环境遗传独特性影响化疗反应
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 肿瘤微环境遗传独特性影响化疗反应
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that genetically distinct Rag1-/- mouse strains with identical tumor cells exhibit varied survival and chemotherapy response, highlighting microenvironment's role in therapy resistance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 肿瘤微环境的遗传背景对治疗反应的影响常被忽视。本文利用遗传不同的小鼠模型植入相同肿瘤细胞，发现不同品系小鼠的肿瘤生长和化疗反应显著不同，证明微环境的遗传独特性独立于肿瘤细胞内在特征和适应性免疫，影响治疗耐药，并揭示髓系免疫区室的关键作用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 肿瘤微环境的遗传背景对治疗反应的影响常被忽视。本文利用遗传不同的小鼠模型植入相同肿瘤细胞，发现不同品系小鼠的肿瘤生长和化疗反应显著不同，证明微环境的遗传独特性独立于肿瘤细胞内在特征和适应性免疫，影响治疗耐药，并揭示髓系免疫区室的关键作用。
